task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2214: [Feature] Gateway Client Refactor — Portkey to Bifrost ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2214` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2214 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: [Feature] Gateway Client Refactor — Portkey to Bifrost (#2214)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2214` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2214" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: [Feature] Gateway Client Refactor — Portkey to Bifrost" body: "Closes #2214\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Migrate from Portkey to Bifrost by updating the OpenAI SDK client configuration to use Bifrost's URL and Cloudflare Access authentication headers instead of Portkey's custom headers - Transform model names at the client layer to Bifrost's provider-prefixed format (e.g., `openai/gpt-4o`) to maintain backward compatibility with all 23 consumer files - Simplify cost tracking to always use local calculation via the existing `estimateCost()` function, removing dependency on Portkey response headers - Clean up config templates to remove Portkey-specific features (semantic caching, fallback strategies) while preserving basic model settings, allowing Phase 2 to add Bifrost-native features
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Model Name Transformation Strategy **Options Considered:** 1. Transform model names to Bifrost format at the client layer, maintaining backward compatibility with existing consumers 2. Require consumers to pass Bifrost-formatted model names, updating all 23 consumer files **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Transform at client layer to avoid breaking changes to consumers and minimize refactoring scope.
Design Choice 2: Config Template Handling **Options Considered:** 1. Strip Portkey-specific properties from configs, keeping only model/temperature/token settings 2. Keep configs as-is but ignore them entirely when sending to Bifrost 3. Remove config template system entirely **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Clean up configs to prevent errors while preserving useful settings. Config templates can be enhanced with Bifrost-specific features in Phase 2.
Design Choice 3: Cost Tracking Without Portkey Headers **Options Considered:** 1. Always use the existing local `estimateCost()` function for cost calculation 2. Attempt to extract from Bifrost headers, falling back to local calculation **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Rely on existing `estimateCost()` function since Bifrost cost headers aren't documented. This fallback path already exists and is tested.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Core Client Refactoring Replace Portkey routing with Bifrost gateway connectivity. This phase focuses on the fundamental connection layer changes while maintaining the existing OpenAI SDK client pattern.
Task 1: Update Gateway Client for Bifrost Connection Modify the simple gateway client to connect to Bifrost instead of Portkey with Cloudflare Access authentication. - Update `packages/ai-gateway/src/gateway-client.ts` to read `BIFROST_GATEWAY_URL` environment variable instead of checking for Portkey credentials - Replace baseURL from `https://api.portkey.ai/v1/proxy` to the Bifrost URL (e.g., `https://bifrost.slideheroes.com/v1`) - Replace Portkey authentication headers (`x-portkey-api-key`, `x-portkey-virtual-key`, `x-portkey-provider`) with Cloudflare Access headers (`CF-Access-Client-Id`, `CF-Access-Client-Secret`) - Read credentials from new env vars: `BIFROST_CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, `BIFROST_CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` - Update `isPortkeyConfigured()` function to `isBifrostConfigured()` checking for Bifrost URL and CF credentials
Task 2: Refactor Enhanced Gateway Client Update the enhanced client to remove all Portkey-specific headers and implement Bifrost-compatible request configuration. - Update `packages/ai-gateway/src/enhanced-gateway-client.ts` to remove all `x-portkey-*` header construction - Remove headers: `x-portkey-api-key`, `x-portkey-virtual-key`, `x-portkey-provider`, `x-portkey-config`, `x-portkey-request-metadata-*`, `x-portkey-trace-id` - Add Cloudflare Access headers to `_createHeaders()` function - Remove config serialization logic that sent Portkey configs via `x-portkey-config` header - Update `_createGatewayClient()` to use Bifrost URL and CF Access authentication - Preserve tracking metadata (userId, teamId, feature, sessionId) as custom headers if Bifrost supports forwarding, otherwise store for local usage tracking only
Task 3: Update Provider Detection and Model Name Transformation Modify model name handling to use Bifrost's provider-prefixed format while maintaining backward compatibility for consumers. - Update `getProviderForModel()` in `packages/ai-gateway/src/enhanced-gateway-client.ts` to return a tuple of (provider, bifrostModelName) - Transform incoming model names to Bifrost format: `gpt-4o` → `openai/gpt-4o`, `claude-3.5-sonnet` → `anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet`, `llama-3.3-70b` → `groq/llama-3.3-70b` - Handle models that may already be in Bifrost format (containing `/`) by passing through unchanged - Update model parameter in API request construction to use transformed model name - Preserve original model name in usage tracking metadata for consistency with existing records
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` Replace Portkey routing with Bifrost gateway connectivity while maintaining the existing OpenAI SDK client pattern. **Update Gateway Client (`packages/ai-gateway/src/gateway-client.ts`):** - Change environment variable reads from Portkey credentials to `BIFROST_GATEWAY_URL` - Update baseURL from `https://api.portkey.ai/v1/proxy` to the Bifrost URL (e.g., `https://bifrost.slideheroes.com/v1`) - Replace authentication headers: - Remove: `x-portkey-api-key`, `x-portkey-virtual-key`, `x-portkey-provider` - Add: `CF-Access-Client-Id`, `CF-Access-Client-Secret` (read from `BIFROST_CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, `BIFROST_CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET`) - Rename `isPortkeyConfigured()` to `isBifrostC
label: neo-issue-2214
